query: ok i'm done with the sync engine changes, push everything up and make sure the PR descriptions are current
i need to split this into two PRs — the proto regen should be separate from the sync logic changes. can you move the proto stuff to its own branch underneath?
my stack is out of date, main moved forward a bunch since yesterday. sync it up and fix any conflicts
create a new branch on top of luan/sync-engine-improvements for the test infrastructure changes
push this but don't create new PRs for the WIP branches, just update the ones that already exist
check the current stack, i want to see what branches i have and their status
the changes to SyncEngine.swift should actually be in the parent branch, not this one. can you move them down?
i accidentally committed the test file to the wrong branch in my stack, can you amend it to the right one?
rebase my branches onto the latest main, there were some conflicts last time so watch out for the proto files
ship it — push all my branches and create PRs for any that don't have one yet
commit these changes with a message about fixing the duplication_index convergence test
the test in SyncEngineTests.swift is failing on line 234, can you take a look? it's the convergence test for profile dedup
review my changes before i submit — i want to make sure the sync batching logic is correct
update the PR description for luan/sync-profile-dedup-index to mention the new convergence tests
stage just the changes to SyncEngine.swift but not the test file, i want to commit them separately
show me the git log for the last 10 commits on main, i want to see what landed while i was out
what does the remoteOriginSyncIDs property do? i see it referenced in the convergence test but i'm not sure how it works
reorganize the commits on this branch — the first commit has both the refactor and the feature mixed together, split them clean
discard the changes to Package.swift, i don't want those in this branch. keep everything else staged
create a new branch for the copy confirmation feature, it's tracked in BRSR-1922
